Package javax.media.mscontrol.resource

This package provides the base definitions for managing media processing resources.

See:
          Description

Interface Summary
Action An Action defines a specific operation on a Resource, like Player.STOP.
AllocationEvent This event is sent when ResourceContainer.confirm() completes.
AllocationEventListener An application must register an AllocationEventListener to be notified of a ResourceContainer.confirm() completion.
AllocationEventNotifier Characteristics of objects able to send AllocationEvent's.
Resource<T extends ResourceContainer> Interface for things common to Resources.
ResourceContainer Gathers the set of methods to control a container of media Resources.
ResourceEvent<T> A Resource sends a ResourceEvent to notify the application.
Trigger A Trigger is a condition that can be used to build an RTC.
 

Class Summary
RTC An RTC (Run Time Control) object associates a Trigger condition with some Action.
 

Package javax.media.mscontrol.resource Description

This package provides the base definitions for managing media processing resources.
They are used by the MediaGroup, MediaMixer and NetworkConnection packages and sub-packages.

Package Details

  • ResourceContainers are defined by Configuration's.
    Specific (enumerated) Configurations are defined by the container's interface class.
  • Run Time Controls (RTC) allow automated resource interactions inside a ResourceContainer, like stopping the Player on reception of a DTMF. An RTC is defined by the association of a Trigger and an Action. Trigger and Action are enumerated items, defined by the Resources that implement them.
  • Many options are provided by enumerated Parameter's. Those items, and their associated values, can be gathered in Parameters classes, and used as arguments in several API calls, or set permanently with MediaObject.setParameters(javax.media.mscontrol.Parameters).
    Parameters are defined by the Resources that implement them.
  • The Resources send events:
    • Each Resource extends the ResourceEvent class with its own Resource-specific methods.
    • A Resource event can use the generic EventType's and Qualifier's defined by ResourceEvent. It can also define its own EventTypes and Qualifiers.
  • The events also contain an error indication: a MediaErr. Resource-specific errors can be defined in the Resource event interface.
